issue.summary: The "legacy" substring in legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts matched the NON_DEFAULT_CONFIG pattern, so the GCM finding was incorrectly dropped as FP
issue.description: Semgrep detected an AES-256-GCM authTag-not-verified finding at src/agents/auth-profiles/legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts:291, but the word "legacy" in the filename matched the "inactive code" pattern and the finding was dropped as FP under criterion ②. In reality this code runs in the default configuration.
issue.reproduction: python tools/semgrep_run.py /tmp/openclaw → legacy-oauth-sidecar.ts:291 LOGIC_BUG raised → 04-A criterion ② marks it FP as "inactive in default config"
issue.root_cause: "legacy" in the filename was treated the same as "legacy" in a code comment or semantic marker. A filename is only a naming convention; it says nothing about whether the code actually runs.
issue.resolution: Under criterion ② check the "legacy" pattern only inside in-code comment contexts, not in filenames. Pattern change: r"legacy" → r"(?:#|//|/\*)\s*legacy" → Added as a note in 04-B fp-patterns.md.
issue.update_note: Add a warning under criterion ② about matching "legacy" in filenames
